description: Full audit and correctness verification of the Pike Language Server. Every test, every feature module, every harness component is fact-checked against ground truth (pike as oracle). Passing tests do not mean correct — tests lie if they are hallucinated or test the wrong thing. Each story verifies one subsystem end-to-end by cross-referencing test expectations against actual pike output and LSP behavior.

userStories.title: Audit test infrastructure — harness runner and snapshot correctness
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the test harness itself is trustworthy before trusting any test results. The harness spawns pike, captures JSON, diffs against snapshots. If the harness lies, all tests lie.
acceptanceCriteria: Read harness/src/runner.ts end-to-end. Verify pike invocation flags match what introspect.pike and resolve.pike expect
Read harness/introspect.pike. Verify every field it outputs matches the IntrospectionResult type in harness/src/types.ts — no missing fields, no extra fields, no wrong types
Read harness/resolve.pike. Same verification against its expected output schema
Read harness/src/snapshot.ts. Verify canonicalStringify produces deterministic output (sorted keys at all levels). Verify diffSnapshot correctly identifies added/removed/changed fields
Verify corpus/corpus.json RunnerOptions (strict, modulePath, includePath) are correctly applied in the runner for each corpus file
Read harness/__tests__/harness.test.ts. Verify test expectations match actual harness behavior — not just 'test passes' but 'test tests the right thing'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: If the harness is broken, everything else is untrustworthy. Start here.

userStories.title: Audit canary tests — verify they catch real regressions
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify canary tests actually detect broken behavior. A canary test that always passes is worse than no test.
acceptanceCriteria: Read harness/__tests__/canary.test.ts. List every assertion and what it claims to verify
For each canary test: manually verify the assertion would FAIL if the tested behavior were broken. Confirm the test is not tautological (asserting what it itself produces)
Verify the corpus files used by canary tests exist in corpus/files/ and match what manifest.md describes
Confirm canary tests run against actual pike output (not mocked)
Document any canary test that does not have a clear failure mode — i.e., a test that would still pass if the underlying behavior broke
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit snapshot-canonicalizer tests — verify diff detection works
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the snapshot canonicalizer tests actually test diff detection, not just round-trip serialization.
acceptanceCriteria: Read harness/__tests__/snapshot-canonicalizer.test.ts end-to-end
Verify each test case tests a DISTINCT diff scenario (field added, field removed, field changed, nested change, array reorder)
Confirm test expectations are NOT self-referential — the expected values must be independently derived, not computed by the same code under test
Verify deepSortKeys handles all edge cases: null, undefined, empty arrays, nested objects, mixed types
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit tree-sitter-symbol tests — verify against actual tree-sitter output
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify tree-sitter-symbol tests compare against real tree-sitter parse output, not hand-crafted expected values that might not match what tree-sitter actually produces.
acceptanceCriteria: Read harness/__tests__/tree-sitter-symbol.test.ts end-to-end
For each test corpus file: run tree-sitter parse on it and compare the actual output against what the test expects. If they diverge, the test is lying
Verify the Pike source files used in tests exist in corpus/files/ and are the same files the LSP would parse
Confirm symbol extraction logic (names, kinds, ranges) matches what tree-sitter-pike actually produces for each construct
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit parser.ts — verify tree-sitter integration and incremental parsing
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the parser module correctly initializes tree-sitter, loads the Pike grammar, handles incremental re-parsing, and manages the tree cache.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/parser.ts end-to-end (133 lines)
Verify initParser correctly loads tree-sitter-pike.wasm from all candidate paths
Verify parse() correctly passes oldTree for incremental parsing and stores the result
Verify deleteTree and clearTreeCache correctly evict entries
Verify LRU cache (server/src/util/lruCache.ts) has correct eviction behavior — fixed max size, evicts LRU when full
Run existing parser-related tests and verify they test real behavior, not mocked parse results
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit diagnostics — verify error detection matches pike compilation output
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the LSP diagnostics feature correctly identifies and reports Pike errors, matching what pike -c actually produces for each corpus file.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/diagnostics.ts and server/src/features/diagnosticManager.ts end-to-end
For each error corpus file (err-*.pike): run pike compilation manually, capture the actual errors, compare against what the LSP diagnostics feature would report
Verify diagnostic severities, messages, and line numbers match pike output exactly
Verify the diagnosticManager debounce, caching, and mode-switching logic is correct (test real timing, not just 'no crash')
Read tests/lsp/diagnostics.test.ts and tests/lsp/diagnostics-verification.test.ts — verify test expectations match actual pike output for each error case
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the most critical correctness story — if diagnostics are wrong, users get wrong error reports.

userStories.title: Audit symbolTable — verify symbol extraction matches tree-sitter AST
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the symbol table module correctly extracts declarations, references, types, and scopes from tree-sitter parse trees.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (1881 lines) — focus on core extraction logic, not just the exports
For 5 representative corpus files: parse with tree-sitter, manually walk the AST, and verify every symbol the module extracts matches what is actually in the AST
Verify PRIMITIVE_TYPES set is complete and accurate for Pike 8.0
Verify scope handling: local variables are not visible outside their function, class members are scoped to the class, imports are scoped to the file
Verify generic type handling: array(int), mapping(string:int), function(int:string) are parsed correctly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: symbolTable.ts is the largest module at 1881 lines. This story may surface bugs just from reading carefully.

userStories.title: Audit definition and references — verify go-to-def and find-references correctness
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify that go-to-definition jumps to the correct declaration and find-references returns all actual usages, not just some of them.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/symbolTable.ts functions: getDefinitionAt, getReferencesTo
Read tests/lsp/definition.test.ts (1145 lines) — verify test cases cover: local variables, function params, class members, inherited members, imported symbols, cross-file references, generic types
Read tests/lsp/references.test.ts (497 lines) — verify test cases cover: variable references, function calls, class instantiation, member access, cross-file references
For at least 3 corpus files: manually trace a definition lookup and verify the LSP would return the correct location
Verify cross-file resolution works: cross-lib-consumer.pike inherits from cross-lib-base.pike, cross-lib-user.pike uses cross_lib_module.pmod
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit completion — verify completion items match available symbols
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the completion feature returns accurate, complete suggestions for the current context.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/completion.ts (929 lines) end-to-end
Read tests/lsp/completion.test.ts (1096 lines) — verify test cases cover: member access, local variables, function arguments, import completions, stdlib symbols, predef builtins
Verify completion items include correct insertText, detail, documentation, and kind for each symbol type
Verify predef builtins (server/src/data/predef-builtin-index.json) are included in completions where appropriate
Verify stdlib autodoc (server/src/data/stdlib-autodoc.json) is correctly rendered in completion documentation
Verify completion does NOT suggest symbols that are out of scope or not visible at the trigger position
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit hover — verify type and documentation information is correct
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify hover responses show the correct type signature and AutoDoc documentation for the symbol under the cursor.
acceptanceCriteria: Read tests/lsp/hover.test.ts (439 lines) — verify test cases check actual type signatures against what pike reports
Read server/src/features/autodocRenderer.ts (918 lines) — verify AutoDoc XML is correctly parsed and rendered to markdown
For autodoc-documented.pike: verify the LSP extracts and renders the //! comments correctly
For stdlib symbols: verify hover shows the correct stdlib documentation from stdlib-autodoc.json
Verify hover for variables shows inferred type (not just 'mixed' for typed variables)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit rename — verify rename refactoring is safe and complete
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify that rename operations correctly rename ALL occurrences and do NOT rename protected symbols (predef builtins, stdlib).
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/rename.ts (260 lines) end-to-end
Read server/src/features/accessResolver.ts (169 lines) — verify access resolution for rename scoping
Read tests/lsp/rename.test.ts (871 lines) — verify test cases cover: local rename, member rename, cross-file rename, protected name rejection, invalid name rejection
Verify buildProtectedNames correctly blocks rename of predef builtins and stdlib symbols
Verify prepareRename correctly identifies renamable vs non-renamable positions
Verify buildWorkspaceEdit produces correct TextEdits with correct ranges (not off-by-one)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit typeResolver and typeResolution tests — verify type inference correctness
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the type resolver correctly infers types for expressions, function returns, assignments, and chained member access.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/typeResolver.ts (278 lines) end-to-end
Read tests/lsp/typeResolution.test.ts (540 lines) — verify test cases cover: return type inference, assignment inference, chained inference, inference failure
For inference-*.pike corpus files: verify the LSP's inferred types match what pike would report
Verify type resolution correctly handles: this_program, this_object(), function returns, constructor results, method chaining
Verify inference failure cases return 'mixed' or appropriate fallback, not a wrong specific type
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit cross-file features — workspaceIndex, moduleResolver, backgroundIndex
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify cross-file resolution works: importing a module, inheriting a class, and referencing symbols across files all resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts (581 lines), moduleResolver.ts (480 lines), backgroundIndex.ts (184 lines)
Read tests/lsp/crossFile.test.ts, crossFileOracle.test.ts, crossFilePropagation.test.ts, importDependencies.test.ts
Verify workspace indexing correctly discovers .pike, .pmod, and .pinc files
Verify module resolution correctly maps import paths to files using -M and -I flags from corpus.json
Verify background indexing does not block or corrupt concurrent LSP operations
Verify cross-file propagation: editing a base class propagates diagnostics to consumers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit pikeWorker — verify pike process management is correct
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the pike worker correctly spawns pike processes, handles stdout/stderr, manages timeouts, and reports errors truthfully.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/features/pikeWorker.ts (554 lines) end-to-end
Read tests/lsp/pikeWorker.test.ts (153 lines) — verify tests cover: successful compilation, compilation errors, process timeout, process crash, stdout parsing
Verify the worker correctly parses pike JSON output and does not silently swallow parse errors
Verify timeout handling: long-running pike processes are killed and the error is reported (not silently returning empty results)
Verify concurrent pike invocations do not interfere with each other
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit remaining features — semanticTokens, foldingRange, signatureHelp, codeAction, documentSymbol, workspaceSymbol, persistentCache
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the remaining LSP features produce correct output for their respective test cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Read and audit server/src/features/semanticTokens.ts — verify token types and modifiers match LSP spec
Read and audit server/src/features/foldingRange.ts — verify ranges match actual code structure
Read and audit server/src/features/signatureHelp.ts — verify parameter info matches function signatures
Read and audit server/src/features/codeAction.ts — verify quick-fix actions are correct
Read and audit server/src/features/documentSymbol.ts — verify document outline matches code structure
Read and audit server/src/features/workspaceSymbol.ts — verify workspace-wide search returns correct results
Read and audit server/src/features/persistentCache.ts — verify cache serialization/deserialization is lossless
For each: read the corresponding test file and verify test expectations match real LSP behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit server.ts main orchestrator — verify handler wiring and edge cases
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the main server.ts correctly wires all feature handlers, handles lifecycle events, and manages shared state.
acceptanceCriteria: Read server/src/server.ts (1074 lines) — verify every LSP handler is correctly registered
Verify document lifecycle: didOpen, didChange, didClose, didSave all trigger correct processing
Verify error handling: each handler catches and reports errors rather than crashing the server
Verify connection lifecycle: initialize returns correct capabilities, shutdown cleans up resources
Verify the server correctly handles concurrent requests (e.g., completion request while diagnostics are computing)
Read tests/lsp/lifecycle.test.ts, error-handling.test.ts, edge-cases.test.ts — verify they test real failure modes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit integration tests — verify end-to-end correctness across subsystems
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify integration tests actually test the full stack from document open to LSP response, not just isolated units.
acceptanceCriteria: Read tests/integration/activation.test.ts, crossFilePropagation.test.ts, error-recovery.test.ts, documentSymbol.test.ts, p2-verification.test.ts
Verify activation.test.ts tests actual VSCode extension activation (or equivalent)
Verify crossFilePropagation.test.ts tests real cross-file diagnostic propagation with actual file changes
Verify error-recovery.test.ts tests that the server recovers from malformed documents without crashing
Verify p2-verification.test.ts (774 lines) tests real hover/completion/navigation against pike oracle
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit performance benchmarks — verify they measure real work
userStories.description: As an auditor, I need to verify the performance benchmarks measure actual LSP operations, not trivial no-ops.
acceptanceCriteria: Read tests/perf/benchmarks.test.ts (183 lines)
Verify each benchmark invokes real LSP operations (parse, completion, diagnostics) on real corpus files
Verify benchmark measurements are not dominated by setup/teardown overhead
Verify benchmark assertions enforce actual performance constraints (not just 'it ran')
Typecheck passes
